name: Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINN) for Fluid Dynamics
target: Traditional CFD simulations for turbine blade cooling are computationally expensive and require dense meshes.
stitched_idea: We propose a mesh-free PINN framework that embeds Navier-Stokes equations directly into the loss function of a deep surrogate model. By encoding the thermal boundary conditions as soft constraints, the model achieves a 50x speedup in heat transfer prediction while maintaining 98% accuracy compared to High-Fidelity LES data.

name: RAG-Enhanced Clinical Decision Support
target: Electronic Health Record (EHR) systems often fail to provide real-time, evidence-based recommendations for rare genetic disorders.
stitched_idea: We develop a specialized RAG architecture that dynamically retrieves latent representations from a curated graph database of 20,000+ rare disease papers. This system augments a foundation LLM to provide clinical reasoning paths that are cross-referenced with live patient genomic data, reducing diagnostic latency from weeks to seconds.

name: AlphaFold-Derivative for De Novo Enzyme Design
target: Designing synthetic enzymes for plastic degradation is hindered by the vast search space of protein sequences.
stitched_idea: Leveraging the structural attention mechanism of AlphaFold, we propose a generative diffusion model that operates on the 3D atomic coordinates of active sites. The model 'reverse-folds' protein backbones to optimize for specific catalytic binding energies, enabling the discovery of PET-degrading enzymes with thermal stability exceeding 70°C.

name: GNNs for High-Throughput Electrolyte Screening
target: Developing solid-state batteries is slowed by the trial-and-error synthesis of solid electrolyte materials.
stitched_idea: We implement a hierarchical Message-Passing GNN that predicts ion conductivity and electrochemical stability directly from crystal graph structures. By training on a multi-fidelity dataset of DFT calculations, the model screens 10^6 potential candidates in 24 hours, identifying three novel thio-phosphate structures with predicted conductivities >10 mS/cm.
